steps.title: Identify the Expected Performance Method
steps.content: The passage establishes a clear expectation: “I was supposed to memorize the whole thing.” This is the key signal that defines what 'cheating' means in context—departing from memorization is the violation.

steps.title: Match the Violation to the Answer
steps.content: When the narrator describes “cheating, looking up to see what notes followed,” she's violating an expectation about how the piece should be performed—exactly what choice A identifies. The other choices either falsely imply competitive advantage (B), invent an unsupported claim about the teacher (C), or misframe the issue as self-deception about ability (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
